dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - May 10, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**Nationals Fall to Marlins in Miami as Hernandez Leads the Charge**

In a dominating performance, the Miami Marlins took down the Washington Nationals 5-2 behind a strong team effort. Heriberto Hernandez led the way with a 2-hit, 2-RBI day, driving in key runs to seal the win. Calvin Faucher earned the victory on the mound, while Josh Ekness closed out the game with a save. Meanwhile, Luis Garcia Jr. and Daylen Lile had strong games for the Nationals.

**Orioles Take Down Athletics in Baltimore**

The Baltimore Orioles edged out the Athletics 2-1 in a low-scoring affair. Gunnar Henderson had a 2-hit game, including an RBI, to lead the Orioles' offense. Chris Bassitt pitched a strong 6 innings, striking out 6 and allowing just 1 earned run. The Athletics' Luis Severino took the loss on the mound.

**Rays Take Down Red Sox in Tampa**

The Tampa Bay Rays defeated the Boston Red Sox 4-1 behind a strong team effort. Junior Caminero homered and drove in a run to lead the Rays' offense. Garrett Cleavinger pitched a scoreless inning, striking out 3 to seal the win.

**Angels Take Down Blue Jays in Toronto**

The Los Angeles Angels dominated the Toronto Blue Jays 6-1 behind a strong team effort. Jo Adell homered twice and drove in 2 runs to lead the Angels' offense. José Soriano pitched 7.2 strong innings, striking out 7 and allowing just 1 earned run.

**Astros Fall to Reds in Cincinnati**

The Cincinnati Reds edged out the Houston Astros 5-0 behind a strong team effort. Spencer Steer homered and drove in a run to lead the Reds' offense. Andrew Abbott pitched 6 strong innings, striking out 5 and allowing just 0 earned runs.

**Twins Take Down Guardians in Minnesota**

The Minnesota Twins edged out the Cleveland Guardians 5-4 in a back-and-forth affair. Chase DeLauter had a 2-hit game, including 2 RBIs, to lead the Twins' offense. Kendry Rojas pitched 3.1 strong innings, striking out 5 and allowing just 1 earned run.

**White Sox Take Down Mariners in Chicago**

The Chicago White Sox defeated the Seattle Mariners 2-1 behind a strong team effort. Randal Grichuk homered and drove in a run to lead the White Sox' offense. Logan Gilbert pitched 6 strong innings, striking out 9 and allowing just 0 earned runs.

**Brewers Take Down Yankees in Milwaukee**

The Milwaukee Brewers edged out the New York Yankees 4-3 in a close game. Brice Turang homered and drove in a run to lead the Brewers' offense. Fernando Cruz pitched 1.1 scoreless innings, striking out 3 to seal the win.

**Rangers Take Down Cubs in Texas**

The Texas Rangers dominated the Chicago Cubs 3-0 behind a strong team effort. Evan Carter homered and drove in 2 runs to lead the Rangers' offense. Jacob deGrom pitched 7 strong innings, striking out 10 and allowing just 0 earned runs.

**Giants Take Down Pirates in San Francisco**

The San Francisco Giants edged out the Pittsburgh Pirates 7-6 in a close game. Heliot Ramos homered and drove in 2 runs to lead the Giants' offense. Tyler Mahle pitched 5.2 strong innings, striking out 8 and allowing 4 earned runs.

**Diamondbacks Take Down Mets in Arizona**

The Arizona Diamondbacks dominated the New York Mets 5-1 behind a strong team effort. Ryan Waldschmidt had a 2-hit game, including 3 RBIs, to lead the Diamondbacks' offense. David Peterson pitched 5 strong innings, striking out 3 and allowing just 0 earned runs.

**Padres Take Down Cardinals in San Diego**

The San Diego Padres edged out the St. Louis Cardinals 3-2 in a close game. Jordan Walker homered and drove in 2 runs to lead the Cardinals' offense. Adrian Morejon earned the victory on the mound.

**Braves Take Down Dodgers in Los Angeles**

The Atlanta Braves dominated the Los Angeles Dodgers 7-2 behind a strong team effort. Max Muncy homered and drove in 2 runs to lead the Dodgers' offense. Bryce Elder pitched 5.2 strong innings, striking out 8 and allowing just 0 earned runs.

**Tigers Take Down Royals in Kansas City**

The Detroit Tigers edged out the Kansas City Royals 6-3 in a close game. Gage Workman homered and drove in 2 runs to lead the Tigers' offense. Kenley Jansen pitched 1 scoreless inning, striking out 2 to seal the win.
